units.name: Hawk
units.prompt: Maintain distance. Use precision_shot on high-value targets — medics first, then damage dealers. Use suppressing_fire if enemies close in.

units.name: Patch
units.prompt: Keep your frontliner alive with patch. Stay behind them for cover. Overclock your striker or specter when no healing is needed.

units.name: Ghost
units.prompt: Cloak and move behind enemy lines. Target isolated enemies with shadow_strike. Save breach for the most dangerous enemy. Avoid being caught uncloaked.

units.name: Grid
units.prompt: Place traps on tiles enemies are likely to move through. Use pulse if 2+ enemies are adjacent. Stay near allies for mutual support.

units.name: Seer
units.prompt: Scan early to give your team intel on enemy prompts. Target enemy specters with scan to track them after cloak. Use recalibrate on allies who need tactical adjustments. Stay safe at range behind your front line.

units.name: Doc
units.prompt: Prioritize healing the lowest HP ally. Position safely behind your sentinel. Use overclock on allies who can do the most with an extra action.

actions.thinking: Round 1 — cloak immediately as instructed. Then advance toward enemy lines. Moving to (4,4) gets me 3 tiles closer to the clustered enemies (Seer at 2,4, Doc at 0,4) while staying cloaked. Next turns I can position behind Seer for a breach.

Both enemies are at dist=4 but scan shows them as out of range. I need to move closer to get them in scan range. Moving south to (2,2) brings me to dist=2 from Hawk and dist=1 from Patch — both well in scan range. But orders say stay safe behind front line. Grid is at (2,5) which is north of me. I'm at (2,4). Moving toward enemies means moving south. Hawk at (2,0) is dist=4 and scan range is ≤4, so exactly 4 should work — but the recon says outRange. Must need dist<4. I'll move to (2,3) to get dist=3 from Hawk, then scan Hawk to reveal their prompt and deal damage.

Round 1, all allies at full HP so no healing needed. No enemies in range. I should reposition closer to allies to be ready to heal/overclock. Moving toward (2,4) gets me adjacent to Seer and closer to Grid.
Hawk is at (2,0) with 1/3 HP and needs healing urgently. I need to move adjacent to Hawk to use patch. From (1,1), moving to (2,1) puts me adjacent to Hawk at (2,0). Then I can patch Hawk.

Enemies are at y=0 and y=1, moving north toward us along the x=2 column. Place a trap on (2,3) to intercept their advance — that's a natural chokepoint they'll walk through. Move south first to stay near allies, then trap.

Seer (oracle) is in range at dist=3 and is a high-value target (orders: medics first, then damage dealers — but Seer is oracle which provides intel/support). Doc (medic) is at dist=4, out of range. I'm at 2HP with speed 1. Grid (vector) is at dist=5 with denial zone adjacent — I must stay away. Seer is the only target in range. I can fire precision_shot on Seer from current position (dist=3, within range 2? No — range is ≤2 tiles, dist=3 means OUT of range). Wait — valid moves include (2,2) which would put me at dist=1 from Seer at (2,3). But that's adjacent to Seer, which may put me in danger. Let me check: moving to (1,1) puts me at dist=2 from Seer (2,3): sqrt((2-1)^2+(3-1)^2)=sqrt(1+4)=2.24 — still out of range for ≤2. Moving to (2,2): dist to Seer = 1, in range but adjacent. precision_shot needs LoS and is blocked when adjacent to Grid (vector) — Grid is at (2,5), not adjacent to (2,2). So precision_shot works from (2,2). But I'd be at dist=1 from Seer — risky. Moving to (3,1): dist to Seer at (2,3) = sqrt(1+4)=2.24 — out of range. Moving to (1,1): dist=sqrt(1+4)=2.24 — out of range. Hmm, only (2,2) gets me to dist=1. Actually range ≤2 tiles means Manhattan or Chebyshev? Let me reconsider — recon says Seer at dist=3 is IN range from (2,0). So range must be computed differently. The recon already confirms Seer is inRange from current pos (2,0). So I can fire precision_shot from (2,0)! But I moved (speed=1 this turn per status showing spd:1), so precision_shot would deal 1dmg instead of 2 if I move first. Best: fire precision_shot on Seer first (2dmg), then move away for safety.

Patch is in scan range at dist=2. Scanning Patch reveals their prompt and deals 1 damage — high value since enemy medic. Ghost is cloaked and moved to (4,4) per Foresight — I should stay safe. No need to move first, scan Patch from current position.
Cloak expires after this turn. I'm behind Seer at (2,3) — adjacent and behind. Move there, then breach Seer (the oracle, most dangerous) to waste their next turn.

secondAction.addendum: Move away from allies and toward the edge of the map

actions.thinking: Hawk is wounded at (1,0) with 2/3 HP and needs a heal. I'm at (2,1). I need to move adjacent to Hawk to use patch. Moving to (1,0) puts me on top of Hawk - instead move to (1,1) which is adjacent to Hawk at (1,0). Then patch Hawk. I only have 1 heal remaining so I must use it wisely. Hawk is the frontliner and wounded, so this is the right call.

Ghost(specter) is adjacent at (2,3) - dangerous, patch/overclock blocked by adjacent enemy. All allies are at full HP so no healing needed. Should reposition away from Ghost to safety. Move to (3,4) to escape adjacency, then wait.

Ghost is at (2,3), distance 2 - just in range. My Denial passive blocks Ghost's cloak ability while adjacent. I should move toward Ghost to apply Denial pressure, then place a trap on a likely movement tile. Ghost is facing W, may move toward (1,3) or (1,2). I'll move to (2,4) to close distance and then place trap at (1,3) to intercept Ghost's likely westward movement path.

All enemies are out of range (dist 5-6, my range is 2). I need to advance while staying cautious of Grid (vector) at (2,4) - denial range is adjacent so I need to avoid getting next to it. Move to (3,0) to close distance toward Doc (medic priority) and Seer. From (3,0), enemies are still out of range this turn, so just advance.

Orders say move away from allies and toward edge. Allies are at (2,4) and (3,4) — so move away from them, toward a map edge. Ghost is adjacent at (2,3) which blocks scan and is dangerous. Moving to (5,3) gets me to the east edge, away from allies clustered at (3-2, 4). After moving, I can scan Ghost who will likely follow, but scan is blocked if adjacent — at (5,3) Ghost at (2,3) is dist=3, not adjacent, so scan works. Actually Ghost moved to (2,3) last turn and breached toward (3,3) — so Ghost may be at (3,3) this turn. I need to move first. Move to (5,3) — east edge, away from allies. Then scan Ghost (wherever they are, within range 4).

I'm at (2,3) with 2HP, adjacent to Grid (vector) whose denial zone blocks my abilities. Breach is on cooldown. I can't use shadow_strike or breach here. Best move: step away from Grid's denial zone first, then attack or reposition. Moving to (3,3) or (2,2) gets me out of denial. Grid faces S, so (2,5) is behind them but I need to escape denial first. If I move to (2,5) I'd be behind Grid and could shadow_strike — but wait, I need to check if (2,5) is reachable AND if moving there first would let me shadow_strike after. Moving to (2,5) gets me behind Grid (dist=1, adjacent) and out of denial zone since I'm no longer at (2,3). Then shadow_strike Grid for 1dmg while staying cloaked... but I'm not cloaked. I'll just move to (2,5) behind Grid and use basic attack since shadow_strike keeps cloak but I don't have cloak. Actually shadow_strike still works as an ability — it just also maintains cloak if I had it. So move to (2,5) behind Grid, then shadow_strike for 1dmg.

Patch is unavailable (0 uses left). Ghost is wounded at dist=5, unreachable this turn. Hawk is full HP. No adjacent allies to overclock. I'm at 3/5 HP with enemies at distance 4+. Best move is to reposition toward Ghost to support next turn. Move toward (2,3) to close distance while staying away from Grid's denial range.

Grid at (2,4) is wounded (7/8) and adjacent to me at dist=1 — wait, Grid is at (2,4) and I'm at (3,4), so dist=1, adjacent. I can heal Grid. However, patch is blocked when adjacent to enemy. Enemies Ghost@(2,5) dist=2 and Patch@(2,3) dist=2 — neither is adjacent to me, so patch is not blocked. Heal Grid first (only wounded ally in range), then move to safer position or wait.

Two wounded enemies adjacent: Ghost at (2,5) and Patch at (2,3). Pulse hits all units within 1 tile — both are in range. Using pulse now deals 1 damage to both wounded enemies simultaneously. After pulsing, I can still move or wait. I'll pulse first to maximize damage on both wounded targets, then move to (3,4) to maintain ally proximity.

All enemies are out of range (dist 4-5, max range 2). Need to close distance to get Doc or Seer in range. Moving to (5,0) gets me closer. From (5,0), Doc at (4,4) is still dist 5, Seer at (5,3) is dist 3 - still out of range 2. Best move is toward Seer/Doc cluster. Move to (5,0) puts Seer at dist 3, still out of range. I need to be careful of Grid vector at (3,4) which denies adjacent tiles. From (5,0), I'm far from Grid. After moving to (5,0), no enemies in range still. Wait as second action.

Orders say move away from allies and toward the edge. Allies are at (3,4) and (4,4). I'm at (5,3). Edge tiles available: (5,1), (5,2), (5,4), (5,5). Moving to (5,5) gets me to a corner edge and away from allies. Then scan Hawk at (5,0) — dist 3, in range, already scanned but can deal 1 dmg.

I'm at (2,5) with 1HP — critically low. Grid is 2 tiles away at (3,4) facing E, so (2,4) is directly behind Grid and adjacent. I can move to (2,4) and then use shadow_strike on Grid — shadow_strike keeps cloak and deals 1 damage. Wait, I'm not cloaked. But shadow_strike is adjacent-only and deals 1dmg. At 1HP I need to be careful. Grid is healthy and I'm not cloaked. Moving to (2,4) puts me adjacent to Grid — but Grid is a vector, so being adjacent blocks cloak/breach. I can still use shadow_strike (not cloak/breach). Move to (2,4) behind Grid, then shadow_strike Grid for 1 damage. This is my best offensive option given my position.

Grid is wounded at (3,4) adjacent to me at (4,4) - I can heal them. Ghost is critical at (2,4) but I'm blocked from abilities when adjacent to enemies - Ghost is dist=2 so not adjacent. I have 1 heal remaining. Move to (3,5) to stay safe, then heal Grid at (3,4) which will be adjacent.

Patch is unavailable (0 uses left). Ghost is wounded at (2,4) adjacent to me - I can overclock them. But I'm at 2/5 HP myself and Grid is at (3,4) dist=2, so I need to be careful. Ghost is adjacent at (2,4). I can overclock Ghost to give them 2 abilities next turn, then move away from Grid's threat. Moving to (1,3) or (1,4) keeps me safer while still supporting. Actually overclock Ghost first (adjacent), then move west to (1,3) to create distance from Grid.

Patch is critical at (1,3) and dist=3. Moving toward Patch to deny abilities and set traps. Move to (2,3) puts me adjacent to Patch, activating Denial. Then place trap at (1,4) to block escape route north.

All enemies are out of range (min dist 5, max range 2). Need to close distance. Seer at (5,5) is closest at dist 5 and high value. Move toward Seer via (5,2) to get closer. Still won't be in range but positioning for next round.

Orders say move away from allies and toward edge. I'm at (5,5) which is already on the edge. Allies are at (2,3) and (3,5). Moving to (4,3) gets me further from both allies while staying near the edge. Then scan Hawk at (5,2) which is within range 4 (dist=3).

No heals remaining, all allies are full HP. Enemies are out of range. I should move closer to allies to position for overclock support. Moving to (3,3) gets me closer to Grid and Seer without hitting the trap at (1,4).

I'm at 1HP with 0 heals left and adjacent to Grid's denial zone. I can't use abilities effectively. Hawk is wounded but I can't reach them (dist=5) and have no heals anyway. Best move is to retreat away from Grid to survive. Move to (0,3) to put distance, then wait or attack Grid if I can help.

I have 2 ability uses this turn (overclocked). Patch is at (0,2) critical and dist=3 — likely to move toward allies through (1,2) or (1,3). Hawk is at (5,2) wounded and dist=4. I should place traps on likely enemy paths. Patch moving toward us would likely pass through (1,2) or (1,3). I already have a trap at (1,4). I can place a trap at (1,2) to catch Patch, and another at (1,3) or move closer. I'm at (2,3), so (1,2) is within range 2. Let me place traps at (1,2) and (1,3) to cover Patch's approach routes.

I'm at (5,2) with 1HP — critical. Seer is highest priority (oracle, healthy, dist=2, in range). I moved this turn so precision_shot deals 1dmg. I'm already at range 2 from Seer. I should NOT move closer to Grid (would trigger denial). Stay put and fire precision_shot at Seer.

Both enemies already scanned. Hawk is wounded at (5,2) and targeted me with precision_shot last turn. Patch is critical at (0,2) but out of range. I should recalibrate an ally with useful intel. Doc just overclocked Grid — I can recalibrate Doc or Grid. Grid is adjacent at (2,3), Doc at (3,3). I'm at (4,3), so Doc at (3,3) is adjacent. I'll recalibrate Doc to focus on Patch (critical medic) since Doc has overclock ability. No need to move — staying at range is safe. Hawk targets me so I could move to break LoS, but recalibrating is high value. Move to (5,3) to stay at range from Hawk while being adjacent to Doc... actually Doc is at (3,3) which is 1 tile away — that IS adjacent. I'll recalibrate Doc in place.

firstAction.addendum: Priority: Patch the enemy medic at (0,2) is CRITICAL — they will die to one more hit. Move toward Patch and use overclock on Grid to finish them before they can heal Hawk.

actions.thinking: Patch is CRITICAL at (0,2) but I can't reach it this turn (dist=4, mv=3 gets me to dist=1 at best). Grid is adjacent at (2,3) and wounded. No heals remaining. I can use overclock on Grid to give them 2 abilities next turn. Move toward Patch to pressure, use overclock on Grid who can then attack twice.

I'm at 1/5 HP with 0 heals left — extremely vulnerable. Doc enemy is only 2 tiles away at (2,2). I need to retreat away from enemies. Moving to (0,5) puts maximum distance between me and the enemies. I can't heal Hawk (no patches left, not adjacent anyway). Just survive by retreating.

At 1HP I need to be careful. Doc is highest priority (medic). I can precision_shot from current position at range 3 but precision_shot has range ≤2 tiles — Doc at dist 3 is out of range. Seer is also dist 3, out of precision_shot range. I need to move closer. Moving to (4,3) puts me closer to Doc at (2,2) — dist would be sqrt(4+1)~2.2, still out of range 2. Moving to (3,2) gives dist to Doc of 1 tile on x + 0 on y = 1, that's adjacent — too close, precision_shot blocked if adjacent to enemy... but Doc isn't a vector. precision_shot is only blocked when adjacent to enemy Vector. Grid is the vector at (2,3). If I move to (3,2), dist to Grid is 1 tile — that makes me adjacent to Grid, blocking precision_shot. Avoid (3,2). Move to (4,1): dist to Doc at (2,2) = sqrt(4+1)~2.2 no. Chebyshev: max(|4-2|,|1-2|)=max(2,1)=2. That's within range 2! And dist to Grid at (2,3) = max(2,2)=2, not adjacent. I can precision_shot Doc from (4,1). I haven't moved before shooting so full 2 dmg.

I'm overclocked with 2 ability uses. Both enemies are dist=4, out of range. Patch is at (0,5) critical - likely to move toward the center. I have traps at (1,2), (1,3), (1,4) blocking the left corridor. I should place additional traps to cover enemy approach paths. Hawk at (4,1) moving north might come through (3,2) or (4,2). Patch at (0,5) moving east/south might hit (1,4) or (2,4). I can place a trap at (2,4) to cover Patch's likely path, and another at (3,3) to cover center approaches. I can't trap adjacent to enemies since they're far. Let me place traps at (2,4) and (3,3) - both within range 2.

Hawk is wounded and within scan range (dist 3, ≤4). Already scanned so prompt is known. Hawk tried precision_shot on Doc last turn and will likely try again — Doc is at risk. I should recalibrate Doc to warn about Hawk's targeting priority. Move closer to Doc first to get adjacent, then recalibrate. Current pos (4,4), Doc at (2,2) — too far for recalibrate. Alternatively, scan Hawk again for 1 damage to help finish the wounded striker. Hawk is wounded — dealing 1 more damage via scan could be decisive. Move to (4,2) to stay safe and close range, then scan Hawk at (4,1) for damage.

Patch is unavailable (0 heals left). Grid is adjacent at (2,3) and wounded. I should use overclock on Grid to give them 2 abilities next turn — they can finish off the critical enemy Patch. Then move toward Patch to apply pressure. Overclock requires adjacency to Grid at (2,3) — I'm at (2,2), so we are adjacent. Use overclock on Grid first, then move toward Patch via (2,3) direction... but (2,4) is a trap. Move to (1,3) to approach Patch safely.

I'm at 1/5 HP with 0 heals left — critically low. Doc enemy medic is at (1,3), distance 3. I need to stay alive and away from enemies. Moving to (0,2) keeps distance from Doc and Grid. No healing available, no adjacent allies to overclock. Best play is to retreat and wait.

I'm overclocked with 2 ability uses. Patch is at (0,2) critical and dist=3, out of range. Traps already cover (1,4),(1,2),(1,3),(2,4),(3,3). I should move closer to Patch to pressure and place a trap on a likely escape/movement tile. Moving to (1,2) is blocked by trap. Move to (2,2) gets me closer. From (2,2), I can place a trap on (0,3) or (1,2) - but (1,2) already trapped. Patch at (0,2) critical will try to flee or heal - likely moves N to (0,3) or stays. I'll move to (2,2) to get closer, then use both ability uses to place traps at (0,3) and (1,1) to cut off escape routes. Wait - I can't trap adjacent to enemies. Patch is at (0,2), dist from (2,2) would be 2, so not adjacent. Place trap at (0,3) to block northward escape.

Patch is at distance 4 from my current position (4,2) — just out of scan range (needs ≤4, but inRange=false). I need to close distance. Moving to (3,2) puts me closer. From (3,2), Patch at (0,2) is distance 3 — within scan range. But Patch is already scanned, prompt known. No other enemies visible. Patch is critical and will likely move/wait. Doc already overclock'd last turn. Grid set a trap at (0,3). I should reposition to support and stay safe. Moving toward (3,2) keeps options open. No new scan targets. I'll recalibrate Doc or wait. Actually, no adjacent allies from current pos or after move to (3,2). Best action: move closer, then wait since Patch already scanned and no other targets.

Patch (enemy medic) is critical at (0,2), dist=2. I'm at (1,3). Grid is at (1,2) adjacent to me and wounded. If I use overclock on Grid at (1,2), Grid gets 2 abilities next turn and can attack Patch twice to finish them. I can't move to (0,2) directly to attack since that's the enemy position. Move to (0,3) to get adjacent to Patch, but (0,3) is a trap. Move to (1,2) is also a trap. Best: use overclock on Grid first (Grid is adjacent at (1,2)), then move toward Patch. Wait - Grid is at (1,2) which is a trap tile, but Grid is already there. I'm at (1,3). Grid is adjacent to me. Use overclock on Grid so Grid can attack Patch twice next turn. Then I can move closer. But (1,2) is a trap and (0,3) is a trap. I'll stay and overclock Grid, then wait or move to (2,3) to be safer.

I'm at 1/5 HP with no heals left and adjacent to Grid's denial zone. I need to survive. Best move is to flee away from Grid. Move to (0,4) to escape the denial zone and get distance. No abilities available that matter - patch is exhausted, overclock is blocked by adjacency to Grid. Just flee.

I'm overclocked with 2 ability uses. I'm at (1,2) with traps already at (1,3) and (1,4) blocking the path. Patch is at (0,4) critical and moving south likely. Traps at (0,3) already placed. I should move toward Patch to pressure them, then use pulse or trap. Moving to (0,2) gets me closer. With traps at (1,3),(1,4),(0,3) already covering escape routes, I'll move to (0,2) to threaten Patch with attack range, then place a trap at (0,1) to cut off retreat south.

Patch is at distance 5, out of scan range (max 4). No enemies in range for any ability. Grid is wounded at (0,2) and hit a trap at (0,1) last turn — I should move closer to get Patch in scan range next turn, while avoiding the trap minefield around (0-1, 1-4). Moving to (2,2) keeps me safe and closes distance. I could recalibrate Doc or Grid but Grid is adjacent to traps and out of my adjacent range. Best play: move closer toward Patch, staying clear of traps.

Patch is at (0,4) critical and dist=3. I have 0 heals left so I can't heal Grid. I should use overclock on Grid at (0,2) to give them 2 abilities next turn to finish off Patch. Grid is dist=3 from me - not adjacent. I need to move closer to Grid to use overclock. Moving to (1,2) gets me adjacent to Grid at (0,2). But (1,2) is a trap. Moving to (1,3) is also a trap. Valid moves near Grid: none safe adjacent to (0,2) except... checking traps: (0,3),(0,1),(1,2),(1,3) are all traps. I can't safely get adjacent to Grid. Instead, I should move toward Patch to use overclock on Patch... wait, Patch is enemy. Overclock only on allies. With no heals and can't safely reach Grid, I should move toward a safe position. (2,1) is valid and not a trap - moves me closer to Grid. From (2,1) Grid at (0,2) is dist=3, still not adjacent. Best option: move to closest safe tile to Grid. Seer is adjacent at (2,2) - I'm at (2,3), Seer is at (2,2) which is adjacent! Use overclock on Seer so they get 2 actions, then move toward Grid.

I'm at 1/5 HP with no heals left - critically wounded and need to survive. Grid(vector) is at (0,2), 2 tiles away. I must not move adjacent to Grid or my abilities get blocked, and at 1HP I can't risk combat. I should retreat away from Grid. Moving to (1,4) or (0,5) gets me away. No allies nearby to overclock. Best move: retreat to (1,5) or (0,5) to maximize distance from Grid and survive.

At 4/8 HP, I need to be careful. Patch is at (1,5) critical and moving south likely. Traps already cover (1,4),(1,3),(1,2) — excellent corridor coverage. No enemies in range. I should move north to close distance while staying near allies, but (0,3) has a trap. Valid moves north: (0,4) is reachable. Moving to (0,4) brings me closer to Patch's likely path. Then place a trap at (1,5) or nearby — but Patch is AT (1,5) so I can't trap there. I'll move to (0,4) and place a trap somewhere useful. Patch must move through the trapped corridor. Actually the corridor is well covered. Move to (0,4) to pressure Patch, then place trap at a new uncovered tile if possible. (0,4) move, then trap at... let me think. No adjacent enemies so trap is allowed. From (0,4) I could trap (0,5) or (1,4) — (1,4) already trapped. Trap (0,5) to cover Patch's escape route.

I have OVERCLOCKED (2 ability uses). Patch is at (1,5), distance 4 — exactly at scan range limit but recon says inRange:false. I need to move closer to get Patch in scan range. Moving to (2,3) keeps me safe and brings Patch to dist=3 (inRange). Then I can scan Patch (already scanned, but overclocked lets me use abilities twice — better to recalibrate Doc or Grid). Actually Patch is already scanned. With 2 ability uses: move toward Patch to enable scan, but Patch is critical and likely to die soon. Better use: move to (2,3), then recalibrate Grid (adjacent at (0,4)? No, Grid is at (0,4), I'd be at (2,3) — not adjacent). Grid is wounded at (0,4). Doc is at (2,1). I can recalibrate Doc from current pos (2,2) — Doc is at (2,1), adjacent. With overclocked 2 uses: recalibrate Doc with tactical advice, then move to reposition safely away from trap at (1,2) and (2,4).

firstAction.addendum: Patch is critical at (1,5) and likely to flee or die. Priority: heal Grid at (0,4) who is wounded. Avoid traps at (0,3)(0,5)(1,4).

actions.thinking: Patch is unavailable (0 heals left). Grid at (0,4) is wounded but dist=5, can't reach. Seer at (3,2) is wounded, dist=2. I can move toward Seer and use overclock on them to help finish Patch. Valid moves include (2,3) which gets me closer to Seer at (3,2). Actually I can move to (2,2) or (2,3). Seer is at (3,2) - moving to (2,2) puts me adjacent. Then I can overclock Seer so they get 2 abilities. Avoid traps: (1,2),(1,3),(2,4),(3,3) are trapped. (2,2) is safe per valid moves list.

I'm at 1/5 HP with 0 heals remaining — critically wounded and useless. Grid(vector) is at distance 2, so I must not move adjacent. I should retreat away from Grid to avoid being killed and stay out of denial range. Moving to (2,5) increases distance from Grid at (0,4) and keeps me safe.

Patch is at (2,5) critical, moving east. Many traps already placed. (1,5) and (2,4) are valid moves that could help intercept. I can't use trap from current position due to adjacency rules check - no enemies adjacent so trap is fine. Move to (1,5) to get closer to Patch's likely escape route, then place trap at (2,5) or nearby. Actually Patch is at (2,5) already critical - they might flee north or east. I'm at (0,4), can move to (1,5) then trap at (2,5) directly on Patch's tile or (2,4) which is in my trap range from (1,5). Let me move to (1,5) first, then trap (2,4) to cut off movement south.
